question: Define a `struct Book` with members `title` (`std::string`), `author` (`std::string`), and `publicationYear` (int). Write a program that declares an array of three `Book` structs, prompts the user to enter details for each book, and then prints the details of all books.

test_cases.input:
The Great Gatsby
F. Scott Fitzgerald
1925
1984
George Orwell
1949
To Kill a Mockingbird
Harper Lee
1960
test_cases.expected:
Enter details for Book 1:
Title: Author: Publication Year: Enter details for Book 2:
Title: Author: Publication Year: Enter details for Book 3:
Title: Author: Publication Year: 
--- Book Details ---
Book 1:
  Title: The Great Gatsby
  Author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
  Publication Year: 1925
Book 2:
  Title: 1984
  Author: George Orwell
  Publication Year: 1949
Book 3:
  Title: To Kill a Mockingbird
  Author: Harper Lee
  Publication Year: 1960

driver: // The student's code is a complete program that interacts with stdin/stdout. An external test runner would execute it.
errors: Mixing `std::cin >> int` and `std::getline` without clearing the input buffer. After reading `publicationYear` with `std::cin >>`, the newline character remains in the buffer. The subsequent `std::getline` for the next book's title reads this leftover newline immediately, resulting in an empty title and shifting subsequent inputs (e.g., the intended title becomes the author, and the author becomes the publication year, which then fails input).
